name: New Albany Housing Authority
description_short: Government housing authority established in 1937 providing affordable rental units and HUD-approved housing counseling to low-income residents of New Albany, Indiana.
description_long:
The New Albany Housing Authority (NAHA) was established in March 1937 as a government agency dedicated to addressing affordable housing needs in New Albany, Indiana. As a public housing authority, NAHA operates as a community development organization and non-profit entity focused on improving housing access for low-income families and individuals in the region.

NAHA offers a range of affordable housing units including 2-bedroom, 3-bedroom, and 4-bedroom rental properties to qualified applicants. Beyond housing provision, the organization provides HUD-approved housing counseling services to help residents make informed decisions about their housing situations. The application process is straightforward, conducted in-person at their office located at 300 Erni Avenue during limited office hours (Monday-Thursday, 8:30 AM-11:00 AM), with online applications also available through their website.

What distinguishes NAHA is its long history of community service spanning over 85 years and its commitment to community involvement efforts aimed at improving affordable housing infrastructure in New Albany. As a government agency rather than a commercial enterprise, NAHA operates without profit motives and focuses entirely on serving the community's housing needs. The organization provides free counseling services, which aligns with HUD-approved housing counselor standards.

Potential applicants should note that NAHA operates with limited office hours and serves a specific geographic area (New Albany, Indiana). The organization's capacity to serve residents may be limited by available units and funding. This is a legitimate public service agency appropriate for those seeking affordable rental housing or housing counseling in the New Albany area, but waiting lists and eligibility requirements may apply.
pros: Established government agency with 85+ years of proven community service since 1937
Offers multiple housing unit sizes (2, 3, and 4-bedroom) to accommodate different family sizes
Provides free HUD-approved housing counseling services to residents
In-person application process at physical office location ensures direct access to staff
Online application option available for convenience at https://newalbanyhousingauthority.org/online-application
Non-profit structure with no profit motive ensures focus on community benefit
Active community involvement in affordable housing improvement efforts
cons: Very limited office hours (Monday-Thursday, 8:30 AM-11:00 AM only) may be difficult for working individuals
Only serves New Albany, Indiana area, not accessible to residents outside the region
Available units may have waiting lists; no information provided on current availability or wait times
Limited public information about eligibility requirements, income limits, or application timeline
Website access issues noted during research, suggesting potential technical or maintenance problems
best_for: Low-income families and individuals seeking affordable rental housing in New Albany, Indiana
Residents needing HUD-approved housing counseling services before making housing decisions
Families with children requiring multi-bedroom units (2, 3, or 4 bedrooms)
diagnosis: NAHA is best for low-income New Albany residents seeking affordable rental housing or professional housing counseling from a government agency. The primary caveat is limited office accessibility (only 4 hours per day, 4 days per week) and availability constraints—interested applicants should contact them directly at 812-948-2319 to confirm current unit availability and eligibility requirements.
services: In-person housing applications at office location
Community housing improvement programs and initiatives
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
